notes: To fix Mouse-Bug I have to run the game in "Exclusive Fullscreen Mod" at the start. In settings > gamesettings disable "Mouse Acceleration" and reduce the "Mouse sensitivity" to 50% will fix the Mouse-Bug to 90% for me. The "Emulate Virtual Desktop" method via winetricks won't work for me.
title: Car Mechanic Simulator 2018

notes: As with everyone else I have the mouse issue, but none of the "fixes" worked for me. This is unplayable for me, but I set it to Bronze because if you have insane amounts of patience it's definitely playable.
title: Car Mechanic Simulator 2018

notes: You have to run it in an "Emulate Virtual Desktop mode" , otherwise the mouse will flick up to the right corner very often, this can be somewhat mitigated by turning off mouse acceleration. Before 3.16.8-beta the mouse moved diagonally downwards and to the right in "Emulated Virtual Desktop mode" but this has finally been fixed and it is now possible to play the game for me. After 30-60 minutes of gameplay the mouse click will not work properly and the game will have to be reset or just 1/10 clicks is registered in game.
title: Car Mechanic Simulator 2018

notes: The game runs perfectly after running it with "Emulate a virtual desktop" turned on. (You can enable it by running winecfg in the wineprefix for the game and ticking "Emulate a virtual desktop" under graphics.) Without it on, the cursor will often jump to the top-left corner.
title: Car Mechanic Simulator 2018

notes: Completely forgot about the codec issue in my previous report. Anyway, the game functions perfectly fine with 'Emulate a virtual desktop' on in winecfg. But there's the codec issue in the tutorial, where none of the videos will play because of missing codecs. Other than that the game runs fine.
title: Car Mechanic Simulator 2018

notes:
Attempted all mentioned fixes for mouse bug but they did not work.  Tried running winetricks on multiple settings individually, running in-between each setting change, using options that had to do with mouse control, clipping, window control, etc. with no luck.  Mouse is constantly moving to top left.  I tried changing to Exclusive Full Screen with no luck, and also emulated desktop mode.  I also tried full screen vs windowed in-game settings, but no difference.  At one point I noticed my regular desktop cursor was still moving behind the window and whenever the real mouse cursor and game window intersected, the mouse pointer went to the upper-left in-game.  So I suspect the issue is with the game window ignoring external mouse input once the game starts.
I am rating borked because the game is simply not playable without suicidal thoughts when the mouse cursor continues to move to upper-left.  Hopefully someone will figure out a way to ignore external mouse input when the game has focus.
title: Car Mechanic Simulator 2018

notes: I fixed the codec issue! You need to install 'Windows Media Foundation' to get the videos to play. There's a script at https://github.com/z0z0z/mf-install that can install it for you. Then to stop the cursor from jumping towards the top left of the screen you will need to enable 'Emulate a Virtual Desktop' in winecfg. With these fixes, the game runs quite well. No other issues that I'm aware of.
title: Car Mechanic Simulator 2018

title: Car Mechanic Simulator 2018

notes: As of Proton 4.11-5 this is now generally playable if you use a controller. Mouse still doesn't work properly (jumps to the top left corner of the screen). Tutorial videos in the beginning tutorial needs codecs from Windows Media Foundation to work. Biggest issue however is that after something like 30m of play the game drops to something like 15-20fps, you can get past this by restarting the game however and it will run again for 30-ish minutes.
title: Car Mechanic Simulator 2018

notes: Victim of the "mouse cursor wants to stay in the upper left corner" problem.  I have tried everything to fix this issue that makes the game unplayable, including upgrading my Ubuntu distribution to 19.04 from 18.04 based on advice in this database.  I use Proton/Steam Play for numerous Windows games and have had zero issues on all of them until I came across Car Mechanic Simulator 2018.
title: Car Mechanic Simulator 2018
